[great-falls-national-park.html] section: Great Falls National Park — The visitor's center is very nice with guides and maneuverable space. Note that the electronic door was broken as of mid-August 2025, but there are guides staffing the center during park hours who could assist with the doors.
[huntley-meadows-park.html] section: HUNTLEY MEADOWS PARK — One of the accessible trails begins at the edge of the parking lot. The main boardwalk for the meadow is 1,904 feet from the parking lot.

[index.html] section: CHOOSE YOUR CENTER — - Andrew O'Dell, SPARC Programmer ​

[huntley-meadows-park.html] section: HUNTLEY MEADOWS PARK — There are directions and information on signs at an accessible height, however they do not list accessible areas. ​There is no Braille on the signs.

[huntley-meadows-park.html] section: HUNTLEY MEADOWS PARK — Unique Feature: Nature Boardwalk Huntley Meadows has a long nature boardwalk that is flat and maneuverable for wheelchairs. The boardwalk features beautiful nature and various wildlife. The best time to visit to see the wildlife is early in the morning.

[belle-haven-park.html] section: BELLE HAVEN PARK — Only the sign for the bathrooms contains the international symbol for accessibility. Maps are at an accessible height, but do not indicate any accessible areas. || [alexandria.html] section: PARKS
[belle-haven-park.html] section: BELLE HAVEN PARK — PICNIC SHELTER/ SEATING AREAS None of the picnic areas, grills, or trash cans are accessible. They are all on the grass.

[belle-haven-park.html] section: BELLE HAVEN PARK — Trails are accessible but not marked There are no resting areas, no shade, and no accessible seating
[glencarlyn-park.html] section: Glencarlyn Park — Unique Feature: Beautiful walking trails Glencarlyn has a long trail system through the woods that runs along an idyllic stream.

[bluemont-park.html] section: Bluemont Park — The trail from the parking lot to the East trail system has one narrow area that is only 4 feet wide. The path from the parking lot to the West trail system is wide and easily accessible.

[glencarlyn-park.html] section: Glencarlyn Park — There are no signs containing Braille. The signs that are at the park are at a readable height for wheelchairs with high contrast text. || [arlington.html] section: PARKS — Bluemont Park - Click here Mostly Inaccessible
[bluemont-park.html] section: Bluemont Park — PARKING There is plenty of accessible parking near the main shelter.
[bluemont-park.html] section: Bluemont Park — There is no visitors center at this park. There is a park service building at the main shelter with a small office only accessible to park staff.
[arlington.html] section: PARKS

[bluemont-park.html] section: Bluemont Park — The whole area has both new and old signs, the newer signs have high contrast color and text, but the old signs don't. None of the signs contain Braille, and some are higher than wheelchair height and could be hard to see.

[bluemont-park.html] section: Bluemont Park — The main shelter is nice and spacious, and the path to the shelter is wide enough for a wheelchair, however watch for some overgrown bushes over the path. The second shelter is down the West path, and has an accessible table.

[bluemont-park.html] section: Bluemont Park — The main shelter is nice and spacious, and the path to the shelter is wide enough for a wheelchair, however watch for some overgrown bushes over the path. The second shelter is down the West path, and has an accessible table.
[index.html] section: CHOOSE YOUR CENTER — Alexandria
[bluemont-park.html] section: Bluemont Park — Unique Feature: Accessible Baseball Field This park has an accessible baseball field and viewing area.

[bluemont-park.html] section: Bluemont Park — There are no accessible bathrooms. The bathrooms are marked with the international sign of accessibility, however there is a step to enter the bathroom and no maneuverability space, and no accessible features in the bathroom.

[mclean.html] section: PARKS — Great Falls National Park - click here Mostly Accessible || [wolf-trap-national-park.html] section: Wolf Trap National Park — The signage is near the main amphitheater and there are no directional signs that guide you to it. ​There is no Braille.
[wolf-trap-national-park.html] section: Wolf Trap National Park — The amphitheater in the woods at the end of the trail also has accessible seating areas. The trash cans are out of reach.

[wolf-trap-national-park.html] section: Wolf Trap National Park — The trail on the left toward the gazebo is partially paved and mostly flat. The trail to the right is rougher and would be more difficult to maneuver in a wheelchair.
[tuscarora-park.html] section: Tuscarora Park — The accessible parking spaces are marked with the international accessibility symbol. ​There is adequate maneuverability around the accessible parking.
[tuscarora-park.html] section: Tuscarora Park — There is an accessible bathroom at the main picnic structure. There is also an accessible porta potty in the parking lot.

[tuscarora-park.html] section: Tuscarora Park — There is an accessible table in the shelter, however it was up against a pillar which would make it difficult to maneuver to. ​The grills and trash cans are accessible.

[tuscarora-park.html] section: Tuscarora Park — There is an accessible table in the shelter, however it was up against a pillar which would make it difficult to maneuver to. ​The grills and trash cans are accessible.

[tuscarora-park.html] section: Tuscarora Park — After about a quarter mile it turns into a bike trail with two lanes so bikes can maneuver around a wheelchair. There are no signs indicating how to get to the main trailhead.
[tuscarora-park.html] section: Tuscarora Park — SIGNAGE - There are not many signs, and there are no signs down to the trailhead which is not immediately visible from the parking lot.
[potomac-crossing-park.html] section: Potomac Crossing Park — There is one fully accessible paved parking space in a gravel parking lot. ​

[potomac-crossing-park.html] section: Potomac Crossing Park — There is a good, wheelchair height, sign at the start of the paved section of the trail. The sign includes slope and cross slope percentages. || [leesburg.html] section: PARKS

[potomac-crossing-park.html] section: Potomac Crossing Park — TRAILS There is a very short section of flat, gravel trail that runs alongside the park, but as soon as it gets to the woods it becomes rough and inaccessible to wheelchairs.

[index.html] section: CHOOSE YOUR CENTER — Alexandria Arlington || [potomac-crossing-park.html] section: Potomac Crossing Park — Unique Feature: Accessible Playground This park has playground features accessible for wheelchairs.
[potomac-crossing-park.html] section: Potomac Crossing Park — There is an accessible grill at the picnic structure. There are accessible trash cans and pet cleanup bags.
[potomac-crossing-park.html] section: Potomac Crossing Park — There is an accessible grill at the picnic structure. There are accessible trash cans and pet cleanup bags.
[potomac-crossing-park.html] section: Potomac Crossing Park — BATHROOMS There is a clean, large, accessible porta potty on site near the shelter.
[huntley-meadows-park.html] section: HUNTLEY MEADOWS PARK — PARKING There are 3 designated accessible parking spaces

[huntley-meadows-park.html] section: HUNTLEY MEADOWS PARK — There is a fully accessible bathroom available in the visitor's center during park hours from Monday-Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM on weekdays, and 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM on weekends. There is an accessible porta potty in the parking lot, it is fairly dirty.
[belle-haven-park.html] section: BELLE HAVEN PARK — Compliant number of ADA parking spaces Limited number of aisles with sufficient width

[martin-luther-king-jr-park.html] section: Martin Luther King, Jr. Park — There is an accessible grill. The trash can is not accessible, however there is an accessible recycling bin near the tennis courts close by.
[glencarlyn-park.html] section: Glencarlyn Park — The beginning of the trailhead starts at the edge of the parking lot. The trails are nice with a lot of shade and beautiful scenery.

[glencarlyn-park.html] section: Glencarlyn Park — There is one fully accessible parking space near the main picnic area. ​Note that there are no designated accessible parking spaces in the overflow parking lots.
[glencarlyn-park.html] section: Glencarlyn Park — ​ ​All of the trash cans are accessible.

[bluemont-park.html] section: Bluemont Park — There are no accessible bathrooms. The bathrooms are marked with the international sign of accessibility, however there is a step to enter the bathroom and no maneuverability space, and no accessible features in the bathroom.

[great-falls-national-park.html] section: Great Falls National Park — PARKING - There is plenty of accessible parking with adequate maneuvering space near the trail entrance that leads to the visitor's center.

[great-falls-national-park.html] section: Great Falls National Park — There are accessible tables, however they are near the bathrooms which, during the hotter days in summer, did not smell pleasant. All of the other picnic tables are on the grass and may be difficult to traverse in a wheelchair.

[great-falls-national-park.html] section: Great Falls National Park — Unique Feature: Accessible Viewing Area of the Falls This park has a specifically designated accessible viewing area of the falls at Overlook 3 .
[great-falls-national-park.html] section: Great Falls National Park — - Note that only Overlook 3 has an accessible viewing area. - The falls are partially visible at wheelchair height at Overlook 2.

[great-falls-national-park.html] section: Great Falls National Park — There are accessible tables, however they are near the bathrooms which, during the hotter days in summer, did not smell pleasant. All of the other picnic tables are on the grass and may be difficult to traverse in a wheelchair.
[great-falls-national-park.html] section: Great Falls National Park — SIGNAGE There is plenty of clear, directional and designated signage throughout the park. || [mclean.html] section: PARKS

[wolf-trap-national-park.html] section: Wolf Trap National Park — There is plenty of accessible parking near the trailhead, but you have to drive past the event parking spaces and through the employee parking to get there. The accessible parking near the trailhead is not available on days when there are concerts unless you arrive

[wolf-trap-national-park.html] section: Wolf Trap National Park — There is a family accessible bathroom with hand rails and maneuverable space. The soap and hand dryer are out of reach for wheelchair height.

[wolf-trap-national-park.html] section: Wolf Trap National Park — There is a family accessible bathroom with hand rails and maneuverable space. The soap and hand dryer are out of reach for wheelchair height.
[wolf-trap-national-park.html] section: Wolf Trap National Park — The amphitheater in the woods at the end of the trail also has accessible seating areas. The trash cans are out of reach.

[martin-luther-king-jr-park.html] section: Martin Luther King, Jr. Park — TRAILS This park does not have any trails, it has a swimming pool and tennis courts.
[martin-luther-king-jr-park.html] section: Martin Luther King, Jr. Park — PARKING There are multiple accessible parking spaces
[martin-luther-king-jr-park.html] section: Martin Luther King, Jr. Park — VISITORS CENTER/CLUBHOUSE There is no visitor's center. There is a pool house but it is only available to employees.
